reasoning: The specific LHCb anomaly in R(K) that prompted this question faded away in late 2022, when a combined re-analysis showed it was consistent with the Standard Model within 1 sigma. Recent 2024 and 2025 measurements from both LHCb and Belle II continue to confirm the Standard Model expectations of lepton flavour universality. There are currently no preprints claiming a 5-sigma violation of lepton universality, and since peer review in experimental particle physics typically takes months, it is practically impossible for a 5-sigma peer-reviewed discovery to be published before the 2025/12/31 deadline. Therefore, the probability of a positive resolution is exceptionally low. I am bounding my estimate at the minimum allowed probability of 0.05.
